title: lagochilus occultiflorus subsp. occultiflorus
intro: Lagochilus occultiflorus subsp. occultiflorus is a plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family, commonly known as the mint or dead-nettle family. It is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of China and other parts of Asia. The medicinal properties of Lagochilus occultiflorus subsp. occultiflorus are primarily attributed to its bioactive compounds, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, and its herbal preparations are often made into teas, infusions, and tonics to harness its health benefits.
